payload.title: R0008: #[strict::macros_ok] suppression broken — attribute stripped before linting
payload.description: R0008 fires for macro_rules! items inside modules that have #[strict::macros_ok] attribute. Root cause: preprocess.rs strip_strict_attrs() removes ALL strict:: namespace attributes (including #[strict::macros_ok]) BEFORE the linting pass runs. By the time NoUserMacrosVisitor sees the code, the suppression attribute is gone. Fix: either (a) lint before stripping strict attrs, or (b) use a different suppression mechanism that survives stripping, or (c) in NoUserMacrosVisitor, check the original source for the attribute rather than the lowered AST. Discovered during RT-12 heck case study.
